tipRules.tip: Hackney: 'At Stick Marsh with no vegetation - just dirt like an AR reservoir - flipped a 1/2-oz Flipping Jig with Rage Craw around willow trees and cane where fish were spawning. Never caught a fish on anything else.'
Hackney: 'Used a Thunder Cricket with a Blade Minnow trailer in bluegill color on a big grass flat where fish were spawning at Lake Garcia.'

knowledge.insight: Hackney: 'At Stick Marsh with no underwater vegetation, fish were spawning around willow trees and cane on the shoreline - they just wanted to have something to spawn around.'
Hackney: 'In MLF, use Score Tracker to decide if you need to abandon a pattern or ride it out. I never got in a hurry. More slow and steady was the deal because fishing was not through the roof.'
Hackney: 'I got behind in the first round but did not worry because it is FL - the way I was fishing you could catch an 8-10 pounder. I like to go places where they have big ones.'
Hackney: 'Flipped mats with a Rodent (black/blue) and a heavy 1.5-oz weight at Lake Garcia and Kenansville. For hydrilla and hyacinth mats, heavy tungsten is essential.'
